title: rubus cerchoviensis
intro: Rubus cerchoviensis, also known as the Cerchov black raspberry, is a rare and lesser-known species of the Rubus genus, native to the Balkan Peninsula in Eastern Europe. It is a type of thorny shrub with edible fruits, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus cerchoviensis include its potential to help lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and even have anti-cancer properties. Rubus cerchoviensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, tincture, or dried fruit, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and other herbal remedies.
